name: Kobold
description: Static-analysis-driven library that minimizes diffing for fast updates

description.value: Declarative Rust web UI library whose view! macro compiles static markup to inline DOM-constructing code and surgically updates only the exact nodes whose expressions change
description.comment: Repo tagline: 'Easy declarative web interfaces.' README highlights the lowest-Wasm-footprint, no-VDOM surgical-update design.

license.comment: Current releases (0.6.0+) are MPL-2.0. Earlier versions were GPL-3.0 (0.0.0-0.1.0) then LGPL-3.0 (0.2.0-0.5.0) before the 2023 relicense.

documentation.comment: docs.rs hosts an extensive crate-level guide; crates.io documentation field points there.

first-release.comment: 0.0.0 published to crates.io on 2021-08-03, 0.1.0 on 2021-08-08 (repo created 2019-11).

latest-stable-version.comment: Latest crates.io release as of 2026-07-19; still pre-1.0.

latest-release.comment: 0.9.1 published 2023-05-26 (checked 2026-07-19); no newer crates.io release since.

maintenance-status.comment: Effectively dormant: last crates.io release was 0.9.1 on 2023-05-26 and the last master commit is 2024-05-05 (checked 2026-07-19). No commits or releases in the last ~2 years, so activity is well below 'actively-maintained'; not archived on GitHub. Solo maintainer.

maturity.comment: Pre-1.0 (0.9.1), client-only with no SSR, no widely documented production users, and no development in ~2 years. Feature-set is coherent for CSR SPAs but the project remains experimental.

corporate-foundation-backing.comment: Solo/community project by Maciej Hirsz (author of logos/ramhorns); no corporate or foundation backing.

reactivity-model.comment: Static analysis at compile time: the view! macro produces opaque static View types; static markup becomes inline JS, and only expression nodes are tracked and value/pointer-diffed on update. Not signals, virtual-DOM, or Elm.

component-model.comment: Components are render functions annotated with #[component]; 'Components in Kobold are created by annotating a render function with a #[component] attribute.'

view-authoring.comment: The view! macro uses HTML-esque syntax with optional closing tags and <!component> invocation syntax.

fine-grained-updates.comment: Updates target exact nodes: 'Changing a string or an integer only updates the exact Text node that value was rendered to.' Static-only views are literally zero-cost to update.

uses-virtual-dom.comment: No virtual DOM; expressions are diffed by value/pointer and the DOM is surgically patched.

async-await-in-components.comment: Stateful hooks expose bind_async, e.g. state.bind_async(|state, event| async move { ... }), enabling async event handlers via wasm-bindgen-futures. No dedicated suspense primitive.

global-state-solution.comment: State is component-local via stateful/Hook/Signal; there is no built-in context provide/inject or global store. Shared state is threaded through props/Hooks manually.

compiles-to.comment: Compiles to WebAssembly via wasm-bindgen/web-sys; static markup also emits inline JS glue for element construction.

primary-build-tool.comment: Examples are built and served with Trunk.

official-cli.comment: No first-party scaffolding/dev-server CLI; relies on Trunk.

dev-server-hmr.comment: Dev loop via Trunk (auto-rebuild + browser refresh); no view/RSX hot reload.

minimal-wasm-bundle-size.comment: README claims 'consistently the lowest Wasm footprint in the Rust ecosystem,' but no reproducible minimal hello-world gzipped .wasm figure under a documented opt profile was found; omitted per RESEARCH.md rather than recording an unmethodical number.

js-interop.comment: Built on wasm-bindgen and web-sys (re-exported via kobold::reexport::web_sys); typed event targets like Event<HtmlInputElement> surface web-sys types.

js-interop-ergonomics.comment: Standard wasm-bindgen/web-sys interop with typed event wrappers; dropping to raw web APIs is the usual boilerplate, neither exceptional nor awkward.

no-std-minimal-runtime.comment: Depends on std, wasm-bindgen, and web-sys; no no_std mode. (Runtime footprint is small, but that is bundle size, not no_std.)

client-side-rendering-spa.comment: Primary and only target is client-rendered single-page apps in the browser.

server-side-rendering.comment: No SSR; the library renders directly into the live DOM on the client. No render-to-string documented.

hydration.comment: Client-only; no server-rendered HTML to hydrate.

server-functions.comment: No isomorphic server-function mechanism; Kobold is a client-side UI library only.

streaming-ssr.comment: No SSR, hence no streaming SSR.

backend-integration.comment: No full-stack/server-integration story; the compiled WASM is static and can be served by any web server, but Kobold ships no framework integration, so none of the tags apply.

isomorphic-routing.comment: Client-only; routing runs solely in the browser.

official-router.comment: A first-party kobold_router crate lives in-tree (crates/kobold_router) with a router example. It is WIP (added April 2024) and not published to crates.io, so it must be used via git dependency.

router-name.comment: In-tree crate providing Router, Params, and a link element; WIP and unpublished on crates.io.

nested-routes.comment: Router example matches flat routes; no nested-layout routing construct documented.

dynamic-route-params.comment: Params exposes get("id") to read parameterized path segments.

typed-routes.comment: Routes are string-based (link route={"/one"}, params.get by name), not derive/enum type-checked.

lazy-code-split-routes.comment: No per-route code splitting / lazy loading.

styling-approach.comment: Styling is via plain external CSS and class names; the view! macro supports a .class shorthand (e.g. <input.edit.edit-head>) and a procedural class! macro. No component-scoped or CSS-in-Rust styling system.

scoped-styles.comment: No built-in component-scoped styling; CSS is global/external.

component-library-ecosystem.comment: No maintained UI component-kit crates; users build their own. kobold_qr is a single-purpose QR widget, not a general kit.

forms-inputs.comment: Inputs are wired to state via bind/bind_async event handlers with value={ref ...}, i.e. controlled; no dedicated two-way-binding or form-helper abstraction.

svg-canvas-support.comment: No first-class SVG support in master; a separate 'svg-attrs' branch exploring SVG attribute handling remains unmerged as of 2026-07-19. The kobold_qr example renders its own widget rather than authoring SVG in view!.

suspense-loading-ui.comment: No declarative suspense/loading-boundary primitive; async loading states are handled manually via stateful updates.

documentation-quality.comment: Thorough crate-level guide on docs.rs (view!, stateful, branching, keywords, events) plus a detailed README and 10 examples; adequate but no standalone book/tutorial series and some areas (async, router) are thin.

api-ergonomics.comment: Statically-typed views with low boilerplate and helpful compiler errors (typos in component fields are compile errors; rust-analyzer refactors into macros). The stateful/Hook/bind! pattern and borrowed-view lifetimes add some ceremony, keeping it short of a 5.

compile-times.comment: The view! proc-macro is comparatively lightweight (emits static inline construction) but still macro-based; no published benchmark, so a conservative 'moderate'.

examples-count.comment: 10 example directories in the official repo (counter, csv_editor, hello_world, interval, list, optional_params, qrcode, router, stateful, todomvc) — in the 10-20 'good' band.

interactive-playground.comment: No hosted online playground/REPL found; only the in-repo examples.

browser-devtools.comment: No dedicated devtools/inspector beyond generic browser tools.

testing-support.comment: No documented user-facing component/e2e testing story; the repo runs its own CI but provides no test harness or guidance for app authors.

crates-io-downloads.comment: All-time downloads of the kobold crate as of 2026-07-19.

contributors.comment: GitHub contributors API count on 2026-07-19; effectively solo-maintained by Maciej Hirsz.

community-size.comment: Solo maintainer with ~4 contributors and 401 stars; a dedicated Discord server exists but the active community is small and the project is currently dormant.

ecosystem-crates.comment: In-tree kobold_router (WIP) and kobold_qr; essentially no third-party ecosystem of routers/component kits/integrations.

notable-production-users.comment: No widely documented production/company users found.

meta-framework-full-stack-kit.comment: No associated full-stack/meta-framework layer; Kobold is a client-only UI library.
